last_result.message: Choose the committed tactic you want to rewind before.
last_result.notice: That rewind choice no longer matches the current committed history. Choose from the current options.

checkpoint_options.label: After call opened / before obligation work #5
checkpoint_options.why_checkpoint: call-local boundary; selecting it keeps the call tactic and removes work committed after the call opened
checkpoint_options.repair_use_when: Use this when the recent local step appears to have introduced the current blocker.
checkpoint_options.after_rewind_next: Probe a smaller replacement step or inspect the current goal before recommitting.
checkpoint_options.effect_if_selected: This will undo committed tactic #5 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

checkpoint_options.label: Before call invariant #4
checkpoint_options.why_checkpoint: call invariant introduction point; later oracle/branch obligations often reveal missing preserved facts or an over-strong equality from this tactic
checkpoint_options.repair_use_when: Use this when the current blocker is an invariant gap, a bad-event fact that should have been preserved, or a local surgery problem after a call. Prefer this over fresh restart when the top-level route still looks right.
checkpoint_options.after_rewind_next: Inspect `call_subgoals` or `subgoal_gap`, then probe a revised `call (_: ...)` invariant or a smaller prefix.
checkpoint_options.effect_if_selected: This will undo committed tactic #4 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

checkpoint_options.label: Rewind to before committed tactic #8
checkpoint_options.committed_tactic: by move=> _; proc; call (LRO_o_ll _); auto=> /=; apply dptxt_ll.
checkpoint_options.repair_use_when: Use this when the recent local step appears to have introduced the current blocker.
checkpoint_options.after_rewind_next: Probe a smaller replacement step or inspect the current goal before recommitting.
checkpoint_options.effect_if_selected: This will undo committed tactic #8 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

checkpoint_options.committed_tactic: by move=> _; proc; call (LRO_o_ll _); auto=> /=; apply dptxt_ll.

checkpoint_options.after_rewind_next: Probe a smaller replacement step or inspect the current goal before recommitting.

checkpoint_options.label: Rewind to before committed tactic #6
checkpoint_options.repair_use_when: Use this when the recent local step appears to have introduced the current blocker.
checkpoint_options.after_rewind_next: Probe a smaller replacement step or inspect the current goal before recommitting.
checkpoint_options.effect_if_selected: This will undo committed tactic #6 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

checkpoint_options.label: Before inline expansion #3
checkpoint_options.why_checkpoint: inline boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this expansion
checkpoint_options.repair_use_when: Use this when broad inlining exposed too much code, hid a call handle, or a targeted inline would keep the proof frontier cleaner.
checkpoint_options.after_rewind_next: Probe targeted `inline{1}`/`inline{2}` or inspect `call_site_options` before expanding more code.
checkpoint_options.effect_if_selected: This will undo committed tactic #3 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

checkpoint_options.label: Rewind to before committed tactic #2
checkpoint_options.repair_use_when: Use this only when the chosen procedure-level route was wrong, not for a local invariant or branch-surgery problem.
checkpoint_options.after_rewind_next: Re-enter the procedure with a different high-level route or inspect `goal_info` first.
checkpoint_options.effect_if_selected: This will undo committed tactic #2 and every committed tactic after it in this node.
